title: What Scripture actually means by self-control (it's not what you think)
caption:
Most men are trying to get self-controlled the wrong way.

You grit your teeth. You set a restriction on your phone. You white-knuckle it for two weeks. And then you're right back.

Here's what Paul actually wrote: ἐγκράτεια — enkrateia. Mastery from within. He lists it last in the fruit of the Spirit for a reason. It's not produced by trying harder. It's a product of being filled.

Willpower is finite. It burns down by 9 PM when the house is quiet and the phone is close. That's not a moral failure — that's a source problem. The man with enkrateia doesn't manufacture his discipline. He's formed by what he loves most.

"I can do all things through Christ who strengthens me." — Philippians 4:13. Paul was writing about contentment. The man who genuinely wants what God wants doesn't have to fight himself as hard as the man who doesn't.

And the fourth step: kill the routes. Cut the access. Get the phone out of the bedroom. Get a man in on your sin. Do what Joseph did — run from the house. The man who controls his environment controls himself.

Self-control in Scripture is a product of the Spirit. Start asking for the right thing.

—

#faith #christianmen #selfcontrol #discipline #scripture #godlymen #manofgod #enkrateia #spirit
theme: He runs his life — his impulses don't control him.
